req.title: Consent management (audit)
req.statement: A patient shall grant or revoke consent for data sharing; revoked consent shall be enforced within 15 minutes. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.
error_pathways: accesses after revoke within SLA denied

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-090 mandates four observable behaviours: (1) consent grant is persisted and returns HTTP 201/200, (2) consent revoke is persisted, downstream caches are invalidated, and access is denied within the 15-minute SLA, (3) an AuditEvent is emitted for every mutating operation, and (4) access attempted exactly at the SLA boundary (t=15 min) must still be denied while access after the SLA window has closed must also be denied. Tests are partitioned as: positive=grant+audit round-trip (EP/STC), negative=data access after revoke within SLA (NEG/DT), boundary=access attempted at t=15min boundary (BVA). Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up their own patient context.

tests.name: test_consent_grant_stored_and_audit_emitted
preconditions: Authenticated patient token available via <patient_auth_token> fixture
FHIR-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
No prior Consent resource exists for this patient (idempotent setup via <ensure_no_consent> fixture)
actions: POST /Consent with a valid grant payload signed by patient token
Assert HTTP 201 and consent id in response body
expected: Response body contains resourceType=Consent and status=active
GET /AuditEvent returns at least one event with action=C linked to the consent id
AuditEvent.recorded timestamp is within 5 seconds of test execution
trace: R-HLT-090: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest, requests, time
from conftest import BASE_URL, patient_auth_headers, ensure_no_consent, build_consent_payload

def test_consent_grant_stored_and_audit_emitted():
    headers = patient_auth_headers()
    patient_id = headers["X-Patient-Id"]
    ensure_no_consent(patient_id, headers)

    payload = build_consent_payload(patient_id, status="active")
    r = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/Consent", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert r.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {r.status_code}: {r.text}"
    body = r.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Consent"
    assert body.get("status") == "active"
    consent_id = body["id"]

    time.sleep(1)
    audit_r = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": consent_id, "action": "C"},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert audit_r.status_code == 200
    entries = audit_r.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1, "No AuditEvent found for consent grant"
    recorded = entries[0]["resource"]["recorded"]
    assert recorded is not None

tests.name: test_data_access_denied_after_consent_revoke_within_sla
preconditions: Authenticated patient token available via <patient_auth_token> fixture
Active consent exists for the patient (created in setup)
A protected data endpoint /Patient/<id>/$everything is accessible under active consent
actions: Confirm data access is allowed under active consent (HTTP 200)
Wait up to 15 minutes (parametrized short wait of 1s in CI with env override)
expected: Subsequent data access returns HTTP 403 or 401 within SLA window
AuditEvent with action=U exists for the revoke operation
trace: R-HLT-090: revoked consent shall be enforced within 15 minutes
R-HLT-090: error pathway - accesses after revoke within SLA denied
R-HLT-090: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest, requests, os, time
from conftest import BASE_URL, patient_auth_headers, create_active_consent

REVOKE_WAIT = int(os.getenv("REVOKE_ENFORCEMENT_WAIT_SEC", "2"))

def test_data_access_denied_after_consent_revoke_within_sla():
    headers = patient_auth_headers()
    patient_id = headers["X-Patient-Id"]
    consent_id = create_active_consent(patient_id, headers)

    pre = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/Patient/{patient_id}/$everything", headers=headers)
    assert pre.status_code == 200, "Precondition: data must be accessible with active consent"

    revoke_payload = {"resourceType": "Consent", "id": consent_id, "status": "inactive"}
    patch_r = requests.patch(
        f"{BASE_URL}/Consent/{consent_id}", json=revoke_payload, headers=headers
    )
    assert patch_r.status_code == 200
    assert patch_r.json().get("status") == "inactive"

    time.sleep(REVOKE_WAIT)

    post = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/Patient/{patient_id}/$everything", headers=headers)
    assert post.status_code in (401, 403), f"Expected denial, got {post.status_code}"

    audit_r = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": consent_id, "action": "U"},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert audit_r.status_code == 200
    assert len(audit_r.json().get("entry", [])) >= 1, "Revoke AuditEvent missing"

tests.name: test_data_access_denied_at_exactly_15_minute_sla_boundary
preconditions: Authenticated patient token available via <patient_auth_token> fixture
Active consent exists; revoke timestamp is controllable via <mock_revoke_time> fixture or service test-hook
Service exposes a test-hook header X-Test-Revoke-At allowing injection of revoke timestamp
actions: PATCH /Consent/<id> to revoke, injecting X-Test-Revoke-At = now - 899s (1 second before SLA)
PATCH revoke timestamp to now - 901s (1 second after SLA expires)
GET /Patient/<id>/$everything — enforcement window closed but still denied per policy
expected: At t=now-899s (just inside 15-min SLA): GET returns 403
At t=now-901s (just outside 15-min SLA): GET still returns 403 (consent remains inactive)
AuditEvent emitted for each PATCH operation
trace: R-HLT-090: revoked consent shall be enforced within 15 minutes (BVA at boundary t=900s)
R-HLT-090: error pathway - accesses after revoke within SLA denied
tests.executable:
import pytest, requests, time
from conftest import BASE_URL, patient_auth_headers, create_active_consent

SLA_SECONDS = 900

def _revoke_with_offset(consent_id, offset_sec, headers):
    import datetime
    revoke_time = (datetime.datetime.utcnow() -
                   datetime.timedelta(seconds=offset_sec)).isoformat() + "Z"
    payload = {"resourceType": "Consent", "id": consent_id, "status": "inactive"}
    extra = {**headers, "X-Test-Revoke-At": revoke_time}
    r = requests.patch(f"{BASE_URL}/Consent/{consent_id}", json=payload, headers=extra)
    assert r.status_code == 200

def test_data_access_denied_at_exactly_15_minute_sla_boundary():
    headers = patient_auth_headers()
    patient_id = headers["X-Patient-Id"]
    consent_id = create_active_consent(patient_id, headers)

    _revoke_with_offset(consent_id, SLA_SECONDS - 1, headers)
    r_inside = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/Patient/{patient_id}/$everything", headers=headers)
    assert r_inside.status_code in (401, 403), (
        f"Inside SLA boundary: expected denial, got {r_inside.status_code}"
    )

    _revoke_with_offset(consent_id, SLA_SECONDS + 1, headers)
    r_outside = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/Patient/{patient_id}/$everything", headers=headers)
    assert r_outside.status_code in (401, 403), (
        f"Outside SLA boundary: consent inactive so still denied, got {r_outside.status_code}"
    )

    for action in ("U",):
        audit_r = requests.get(
            f"{BASE_URL}/AuditEvent",
            params={"entity": consent_id, "action": action},
            headers=headers
        )
        assert len(audit_r.json().get("entry", [])) >= 1, f"Missing AuditEvent action={action}"

tests.name: test_consent_grant_then_revoke_state_transitions_and_audit_chain
preconditions: No prior Consent resource for this patient
actions: GET /AuditEvent?entity=<id> to retrieve full audit chain
expected: AuditEvent chain contains exactly two entries: action=C and action=U in order
Each AuditEvent references the same consent resource id
trace: R-HLT-090: patient shall grant or revoke consent
R-HLT-090: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation (end-to-end chain)
tests.executable:
import pytest, requests, time
from conftest import BASE_URL, patient_auth_headers, ensure_no_consent, build_consent_payload

def test_consent_grant_then_revoke_state_transitions_and_audit_chain():
    headers = patient_auth_headers()
    patient_id = headers["X-Patient-Id"]
    ensure_no_consent(patient_id, headers)

    grant_r = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/Consent",
        json=build_consent_payload(patient_id, status="active"),
        headers=headers
    )
    assert grant_r.status_code == 201
    consent_id = grant_r.json()["id"]
    assert grant_r.json()["status"] == "active"

    revoke_r = requests.patch(
        f"{BASE_URL}/Consent/{consent_id}",
        json={"resourceType": "Consent", "id": consent_id, "status": "inactive"},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert revoke_r.status_code == 200
    assert revoke_r.json()["status"] == "inactive"

    time.sleep(1)
    audit_r = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/AuditEvent", params={"entity": consent_id}, headers=headers
    )
    assert audit_r.status_code == 200
    entries = audit_r.json().get("entry", [])
    actions = [e["resource"]["action"] for e in entries]
    assert "C" in actions, "Missing create AuditEvent"
    assert "U" in actions, "Missing update/revoke AuditEvent"
    for e in entries:
        refs = [ref["what"]["reference"] for ref in e["resource"].get("entity", [])]
        assert any(consent_id in ref for ref in refs), "AuditEvent entity ref mismatch"

tests.name: test_consent_revoke_without_prior_grant_returns_error
preconditions: No active Consent resource exists for this patient
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome or error descriptor
No AuditEvent of type U is created for the nonexistent resource
trace: R-HLT-090: Decision Table - revoke without active consent is an invalid transition
tests.executable:
import pytest, requests, uuid
from conftest import BASE_URL, patient_auth_headers

def test_consent_revoke_without_prior_grant_returns_error():
    headers = patient_auth_headers()
    patient_id = headers["X-Patient-Id"]
    fake_id = f"nonexistent-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}"

    revoke_r = requests.patch(
        f"{BASE_URL}/Consent/{fake_id}",
        json={"resourceType": "Consent", "id": fake_id, "status": "inactive"},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert revoke_r.status_code in (404, 409), (
        f"Expected 404 or 409, got {revoke_r.status_code}: {revoke_r.text}"
    )
    body = revoke_r.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome" or "error" in body, (
        "Expected OperationOutcome or error descriptor in response"
    )

    audit_r = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": fake_id, "action": "U"},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert audit_r.status_code == 200
    entries = audit_r.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) == 0, "No U AuditEvent should exist for nonexistent consent"
